title: jacobaea vulgaris var. dunensis
intro: Jacobaea vulgaris var. dunensis, also known as the Corn Marigold, is a variety of the Jacobaea vulgaris plant, which is a perennial herb native to the Cornish Peninsula in England. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The Corn Marigold is also believed to have health benefits such as soothing digestive issues and reducing anxiety, and it has been used in herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, and infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties. The plant's flowers and leaves can be used in herbal preparations, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its effects and provide a range of health benefits.
